points: Air is a mixture of gases (N2, O2, AR, CO2) essential for survival. Oxygen (21%) is needed for all bodily functions.
Water is cover 2/3 of Earth; saline oceans contain most of it. Fresh water is found in rivers, lakes, and underground (precious).
World Water Day is observed on 22nd March; conservation methods like Rainwater Harvesting (Bawadi, Vav) are crucial.
The Sun is the main source of energy on Earth, supporting plants and all life forms; used for heat, light, and solar power.
Forests provide shelter, products, and environmental products; festivals like Van Mahotsav (July) and movements like Chipko are key for preservation.
Soil takes thousands of years to form; rocks and minerals are vital for construction, tools, and modern electronics.
Fossil fuels (Coal, Petroleum, Natural Gas) are non-renewable and polluting; CNG and renewable alternatives (Sun, Wind) are preferred.
Renewable resources (Sun, Air, Water) replenish quickly; Non-renewable (Minerals, Fossil Fuels) found in limited quantities.
